forecaster_note: Solid! OH to 2x OH surf for the open exposures, with S-magnets into the well-overhead to 2x-overhead-plus range. Swell is trending SE and coming in at a solid size — most breaks head-to-overhead, the sheltered southern corners more doable at chest-to-head-high. S'lies mean those sheltered corners are the cleanest option. INCOMING: a solid winter swell holds — Wed decent morning options with PM OK too (OH surf mostly), Thu a slow fade as S/SE backs off, Fri exposures cleaning up as winds swing W, and the weekend eases to fun offshore-morning size. — Hugh McDowell (Surfline)

wind.summary: S 17kph (31 gusts) — cross-shore on the NE beaches, straight onshore on the S-facing reefs. Lumpy on the open banks; the sheltered southern corners are the clean play.

shark.summary: No shark detections at your tracked breaks (Dee Why, Long Reef, Curl Curl, Manly) or anywhere on the Northern Beaches in the past 24h. The only NSW detection today was a 3.15m tiger shark tagged & released off a SMART drumline at Pambula (far south coast). Still, plenty of S-swell energy pushing marine life around — scan the lineup and check the flags.

days.label: Today
days.note: Big solid day gone by — 6–8ft+ and well overhead across the exposures on a SE/S swell (9.9ft @ 11s SE 132°, 1132kJ at Dee Why), but S winds kept it cross-to-onshore and lumpy on the open banks. The clean play was the sheltered southern corners: Dee Why Point for frontside rights, Curl Curl's south end. Day's done now — the real prize is Wed's cleaner overhead morning.

dee-why.window: The best of the four — SE 132° energy suits the bank and the wind sits cross-shore rather than dead onshore. The Point was the pick for clean frontside rights; open bank fun but lumpy on the sets.

tip.label: Point over beachbreak
tip.text: 1132kJ and 100/100 consistency means relentless sets, and SE 132° is a workable angle here (not the dead-S wrap that ruins it). In the S wind the Point shelters best and hands you clean frontside rights — that was the call today.

long-reef.window: Biggest and rawest — a huge 1482kJ of S/SE swell straight onto the outer reefs at 3x overhead, but the S wind is dead onshore and it was a mess. Experienced crew, dawn only.

tip.label: Too much, too messy
tip.text: Long Reef soaks up every scrap of this swell — 1482kJ, 6–8ft+ — but the wind sits straight onshore here. Big and disorganised, not the makeable stuff you want. Bookmark it for a cleaner-wind morning.

curl-curl.window: S-magnet fully loaded (1152kJ) but onshore-affected. The south end / Curl Curl corner off the rocks was the cleaner nook for frontside rights.

tip.label: South corner or skip
tip.text: Curly eats S swell so the size was all there — 1152kJ — but the open beach was onshore and lumpy. The south-end rights off the rocks (your frontside) were the only tidy option.

manly.window: Smallest and most wind-sheltered — 4–5ft on the sets with cross-shore wind. Cleaner than the S-facing beaches but soft. The mellow backup.

tip.label: Sheltered but softer
tip.text: North-facing Manly saw the least of this SE swell — 1028kJ, 4–5ft — but it's the most wind-protected corner. Fine for a clean paddle; for real size and punch the S magnets had it, just messier.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: The standout clean-ish day — Hugh's calling decent morning options with the PM OK too, overhead surf mostly as the S/SE swell holds and winds back off a touch. Dee Why 4–6ft, the reefs a solid 6–8ft. Your 10–12 window should score size with more shape than today; dawn for the glassiest before any S'ly nudges back.

dee-why.window: Quality morning — 4–6ft with cleaner winds. The Point and open banks both on offer; this is the day for size with shape. Early is glassiest.

long-reef.window: Serious size on the outer reefs — 6–8ft. Experienced crew, dawn before any S'ly returns. Step-up territory.

curl-curl.window: Loaded S-magnet at 4–6ft with a cleaner morning — the south corner for the more makeable walls.

manly.window: Holds real size — 4–5ft head-high-plus. Fun rippable option if the reefs feel too heavy.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: The week's big S/SE swell fades by the weekend. Saturday: energy eases to 2–3ft but an offshore morning window means clean, fun-size surf across the region — your kind of Saturday session. Sunday drops smaller (1–2ft) with all-day offshores, crisp and clean but groveller territory. Sat AM is the pick.

dee-why.window: Sat: clean 2–3ft in the offshore morning window — fun rippable session on the daily driver. Sun smaller. Punchy-beachie territory.

long-reef.window: Sat: 2–3ft+ of residual S energy, cleanest early. Sun eases to 1–3ft.

curl-curl.window: Sat: 2–3ft clean in the morning window — the S-magnet holds a touch more. Sun small.

manly.window: Sat: mellow 2–3ft, most sheltered. Sun 1–2ft — grovel only.
